question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 25.00% on 2026-04-23 for the Metaculus question "Will the EU require mandatory age verification on social media or AI before 2027?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the CP on Metaculus question #41173 (EU mandatory age verification before 2027) will be above 25% on April 23, 2026. The CP is currently exactly at 25.00% as of April 13 - right at the threshold. The CP has shown a clear downward trend over the past 5 weeks (from ~28-30% to 25%), reflecting forecasters' increasing belief that EU-level mandatory legislation is unlikely within the remaining ~8.5 months. Breaking news today (April 15) about the EU Commission launching an age verification app could temporarily bump forecasts upward, but analysis confirms this is a voluntary tool under existing DSA enforcement, not a new regulation or directive. No EU regulation/directive is in the legislative pipeline. The 801-forecaster base provides stability. The question is finely balanced because CP is exactly at the threshold, but the downward trend and fundamentals favor continued decline. I estimate ~42% probability the CP will be strictly above 25% on April 23.

factors.description: CP exactly at 25% threshold - right at the boundary, so small movements in either direction determine resolution
Clear downward trend: CP declined from 28-30% range to 25% over past 5 weeks, consistent decline suggesting forecasters increasingly believe EU regulation unlikely before 2027

Major news today (April 15): EU Commission announced age verification app with strong headlines. Could attract forecasters who interpret this as progress toward mandatory age verification, potentially nudging CP up temporarily

The EU app is VOLUNTARY, not a mandatory regulation/directive. Enforcement is under existing DSA, no new legislation. Informed forecasters will recognize this doesn't change the underlying question's likelihood

No EU regulation/directive is in the legislative pipeline. Only a non-binding EP resolution from Nov 2025. EU legislative process typically takes 1-3 years minimum, making adoption before Jan 2027 extremely unlikely
801 forecasters on underlying question - large forecaster base means more stability and less sensitivity to individual forecast updates or news events

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.42 (42% probability CP will be above 25% on April 23)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **CP at exact threshold**: The CP is currently 25.00% — right at the boundary. Any small movement matters.
- **Downward trend**: CP declined from 28-30% to 25% over 5 weeks, indicating forecasters increasingly doubt EU legislation by Jan 2027.
- **Breaking news today**: The EU Commission announced an age verification app with prominent headlines ("EU launches age verification"). This could cause some forecasters to briefly nudge predictions upward.
- **But the app is voluntary**: Multiple authoritative sources (IAPP, IEU Monitoring, EC's own statements) confirm this is voluntary infrastructure under existing DSA enforcement — not a new regulation or directive. Informed forecasters will see through the headlines.
- **No legislation in pipeline**: Only a non-binding EP resolution exists. The EU legislative process takes 1-3 years minimum, making adoption of a mandatory regulation/directive before Jan 2027 extremely unlikely.
- **Large forecaster base (801)**: Provides stability against noise.

The 42% reflects genuine knife-edge uncertainty: the CP is right at the threshold, and today's news introduces a plausible upward shock, but the fundamental trend and legislative reality favor continued decline below 25%.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41173) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-23 23:52:01 is higher than 25.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 25.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-23 23:52:01, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
